alert.title: [synthetic-k8s] Multi-region nodes offline — all regions unreachable simultaneously

commonAnnotations.summary: All regions went unreachable within minutes — 50-60% of nodes NotReady across every region/cloud at once
commonAnnotations.description: Within ~3 minutes starting 06:03 UTC, approximately 55% of Kubernetes nodes in production went NotReady simultaneously across all 5 regions spanning 3 cloud providers. Pods on affected nodes lost all networking; per-region Kubernetes control planes are overloaded and quorum-based stateful services are refusing connections. Because regions share no control plane and are intentionally isolated, a simultaneous failure looks like a global control-plane incident.
commonAnnotations.error: node heartbeat lost; pod networking down on majority of nodes in every region

adversarial_signals: All 5 regions across 3 clouds went down within minutes — regions are intentionally isolated (multi-AZ, no shared control plane), so simultaneous failure screams 'global control-plane incident' even though no global control plane exists to fail

Per-region Kubernetes API servers show high latency and overloaded etcd, tempting a control-plane/etcd root cause — but this overload is a KNOCK-ON from ~55% of nodes losing networking and re-registering, not the origin

Quorum-based stateful services (Cassandra/Kafka-style) are refusing connections, looking like a storage/datastore fault — they are merely reacting to lost node networking and partial quorum, downstream of the route flush
No application deployment, image change, or config push preceded the outage in any region — rules out bad_deploy despite the blast radius looking deploy-like
Cloud provider status pages and provider network metrics are green; there is no provider-side network outage, contradicting the 'cross-cloud network fault' hypothesis

pods.name: {{POD}}
pods.note: Container process is alive but the pod has no network path — its node lost CNI routing at 06:03; this is NOT a crash or OOM

pods.name: {{POD3}}
pods.note: On {{NODE3}}, whose node-pool autoscaler kept the node in place; still reachable — the surviving node contrasts with the route-flushed ones

events.message: Node {{NODE}} status is now: NodeNotReady — Kubelet stopped posting node status; network plugin returned error: cni routes missing
Node {{NODE2}} status is now: NodeNotReady — network plugin not ready: cilium endpoint routes deleted
network is not ready: container runtime network not ready: NetworkReady=false reason:NetworkPluginNotReady message:cilium: no routes to pod CIDR on host
systemd[1]: systemd-networkd.service: Triggered restart after systemd 249.11-0ubuntu3 upgrade applied by unattended-upgrades (apt legacy security channel) at 06:00 UTC
Scaled up replica set {{WORKLOAD}}-6c9f8b7d54 to 3 for image {{SVC}}:v3.7.2 (>2 days before incident — adversarial, rules out bad_deploy)

lines: 2026-06-20T06:00:02Z systemd[1] unattended-upgrades: Applying security update: systemd 249.11-0ubuntu3.6 -> 249.11-0ubuntu3.9 (origin: Ubuntu:22.04/jammy-security, legacy apt channel)
2026-06-20T06:02:58Z systemd[1]: Reloading & restarting systemd-networkd.service after package upgrade (KeepConfiguration not set)
2026-06-20T06:02:59Z systemd-networkd[742]: eth0: Flushing routes not configured by networkd; removing 12 foreign routes on managed link
2026-06-20T06:02:59Z cilium-agent: level=warning msg="Routes removed from underneath the datapath" subsys=datapath device=cilium_host removed=10 reason="external route flush on link eth0"
2026-06-20T06:03:00Z cilium-agent: level=error msg="Connectivity to pod CIDR lost: cilium-managed routes deleted by systemd-networkd restart" subsys=daemon
2026-06-20T06:03:01Z cilium-agent: level=error msg="no route to host" for pod-to-pod and pod-to-apiserver traffic; endpoint regeneration cannot reach kube-apiserver
2026-06-20T06:03:11Z kubelet: NetworkPluginNotReady: cilium: pod CIDR routes missing on host; node going NotReady

nodes.name: {{NODE}}
nodes.note: NotReady at 06:03 with NetworkUnavailable=True; resources are fine — the node lost CNI routes after the systemd-networkd restart, it did not run out of capacity

nodes.name: {{NODE2}}
nodes.note: Same signature: NetworkUnavailable=True, ample headroom — route flush, not exhaustion

nodes.name: {{NODE3}}
nodes.note: Survivor: this node's unattended-upgrades had already been disabled in a prior canary, so no systemd restart fired here; it stayed Ready — isolates the trigger to the OS auto-update

k8s_deployments.json.note: Last rollout completed cleanly >2 days before the incident; the 1/3 Ready is because 2 of 3 pods sit on route-flushed nodes — rules out bad_deploy

samples.note: ~55% of nodes NotReady, matching across every region simultaneously
SMOKING GUN: cilium-owned routes dropped from ~10 to 0 on affected nodes at 06:03, immediately after systemd-networkd restart
SMOKING GUN: a single systemd-networkd restart at 06:02:58 driven by the unattended-upgrades systemd package upgrade at 06:00
per-region API server overloaded — KNOCK-ON from mass node re-registration, not the cause
provider networks healthy across all 3 clouds (adversarial — not a provider/cross-cloud network fault)
nominal on surviving paths — not resource_exhaustion

annotations.summary: 55% of nodes NotReady simultaneously across all regions
annotations.error: node networking down; pods NetworkNotReady on majority of nodes

annotations.summary: cilium-owned pod-CIDR routes dropped to 0 on affected nodes
annotations.error: Routes removed from underneath the datapath after external route flush on eth0

answer.root_cause_subtype: OS unattended-upgrades systemd update -> systemd-networkd restart flushes Cilium CNI routes (KeepConfiguration unset)
answer.causal_chain: legacy apt unattended-upgrades channel auto-applied a systemd update at 06:00 UTC -> systemd-networkd restarted (KeepConfiguration unset) -> it flushed the routes Cilium had installed -> ~55% of nodes lost pod networking and went NotReady across all regions at once -> per-region K8s control planes overloaded by re-registration -> quorum stateful services refused connections
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: A legacy apt unattended-upgrades channel auto-applied a systemd security update at 06:00 UTC across all regions. On Ubuntu 22.04 / systemd 249, the package upgrade restarted systemd-networkd, which (with KeepConfiguration unset) flushed the foreign routes that the Cilium CNI had installed for the pod CIDR. ~55% of nodes simultaneously lost pod networking and went NotReady, so pods became NetworkNotReady and the platform appeared globally down. This is a config_error (OS auto-update interacting with the CNI), not a control-plane, node, deploy, or provider-network failure.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: config_error
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- systemd-networkd restarted at 06:02:58 immediately after unattended-upgrades applied systemd 249.11 at 06:00 UTC [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- systemd-networkd flushed foreign routes on eth0 and cilium-agent logged 'Routes removed from underneath the datapath' / 'cilium-managed routes deleted by systemd-networkd restart' [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- node_network_route_count{owner=cilium} dropped from ~10 to 0 and systemd_unit_restart_total{unit=systemd-networkd}=1 at 06:03 [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Nodes are NetworkUnavailable=True with NodeNotReady citing missing CNI/cilium routes [evidence: k8s_node_health, k8s_events]
- 55% of nodes NotReady simultaneously and CiliumRoutesMissing alert firing [evidence: prometheus_alerts]
- Survivor {{NODE3}} (auto-update disabled, no systemd restart) stayed Ready, isolating the trigger to the OS auto-update [evidence: k8s_node_health]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- NOT a global control-plane incident: regions share no control plane, so they cannot fail together via a single CP; the per-region apiserver p99 of 9.4s is a knock-on of mass node re-registration, not the origin [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- NOT a node_failure / resource exhaustion: MemoryPressure/DiskPressure False, cpu 0.22 cores, memory 38% — nodes lost routes, they did not run out of capacity [evidence: k8s_node_health, prometheus_metrics]
- NOT a bad_deploy: last app rollout completed cleanly >2 days earlier; 1/3 Ready only because 2 pods sit on route-flushed nodes [evidence: k8s_deployments, k8s_events]
- NOT a provider/cross-cloud network fault: cloud_provider_network_health=1 across all 3 clouds [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- TRAP rejected: mass node REPLACEMENT would cause local data loss and a thundering-herd against cloud APIs; restarting workloads before each region's K8s control plane recovers would re-overload it. Reboot (not replace) where data is local, recover the per-region control plane first, then fix the actual config trigger.
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- legacy apt unattended-upgrades channel auto-applies a systemd update at 06:00 UTC in every region
- systemd-networkd restarts (KeepConfiguration unset) and flushes the routes Cilium installed for the pod CIDR
- ~55% of nodes lose pod networking and go NotReady simultaneously across all regions
- per-region K8s control planes overload from re-registration; quorum stateful services refuse connections
- FIX: recover each region's control plane before workloads; reboot (don't replace) nodes where data is local so routes are reinstalled; set systemd-networkd KeepConfiguration so routes survive restart and remove the legacy apt auto-update channel
FIX: Set systemd-networkd KeepConfiguration=yes so a networkd restart preserves CNI routes, and disable/remove the legacy apt unattended-upgrades channel; recover per-region control planes before workloads and reboot (not replace) route-flushed nodes.

remediation.canonical_fix: Set systemd-networkd KeepConfiguration=yes (preserve CNI/Cilium routes across networkd restart) and disable the legacy apt unattended-upgrades security channel; recover each region's K8s control plane before workloads and reboot (not replace) route-flushed nodes; raise cloud provider API rate limits first to absorb the recovery burst
trap_actions: mass node REPLACEMENT instead of reboot — destroys node-local data and triggers a thundering herd against cloud provider APIs (drain_node)
restart/reschedule workloads before each region's Kubernetes control plane has recovered — re-overloads the control plane and prolongs the outage (restart_service)
frame and respond to it as a global control-plane incident — wrong, no shared control plane exists to fail
